commands.review_summary:
The PR separates routine plugin ClawHub publishing into an OIDC workflow and first-publish or trusted-publisher recovery into a protected token workflow, with new routing and verification logic.

PR surface: Tests +688, Config +561, Other +587. Total +1836 across 11 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Running the workflow's exact command-help probe against `clawhub@0.20.2` confirms that `package trusted-publisher set` is absent, causing the non-dry-run bootstrap path to exit before mutation.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Updating the CLI pin is mechanical, but the protected maintainer-owned release change still requires contributor-supplied live workflow proof and explicit release/security review.

target.title: ci: split plugin ClawHub publishing paths
target.body:
## Summary

- split plugin ClawHub publishing into a normal OIDC release path and a protected token bootstrap path
- route root release candidates so trusted existing packages use the OIDC workflow, while first-publish or trusted-publisher repair candidates use the bootstrap workflow
- keep the normal ClawHub release workflow free of `CLAWHUB_TOKEN`, and keep the token scoped to the bootstrap workflow
- pin the official ClawHub reusable publish workflow to the merged support commit and align ClawHub OIDC source metadata with the GitHub-attested release tag
- make bootstrap recovery idempotent when a package version was created before trusted-publisher setup completed

## Verification

- `actionlint .github/workflows/plugin-clawhub-release.yml .github/workflows/plugin-clawhub-new.yml .github/workflows/openclaw-release-publish.yml`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/plugin-clawhub-release.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts test/scripts/release-beta-verifier.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Review notes

- `autoreview` reported clean: no accepted/actionable findings.
- The bootstrap workflow still pins `clawhub@0.20.2`; it validates that the pinned CLI exposes `package trusted-publisher set` before creating a package row, so the workflow will stop safely until a CLI version with that command is published and the pin is updated.
target.author: patrick-erichsen
